channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Artemis 2 Astronauts Plan to Capture Earthrise and Earthset During Moon Flyby
item.description: The Artemis 2 crew, consisting of NASA astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Canadian Space Agency astronaut Jeremy Hansen, will attempt to recreate the iconic 'Earthrise' photog...

item.title: Kenya's Fuel Reserves Dwindle Amidst Middle East Tensions
item.description: Things are a bit tight, manze! Kenya’s super petrol reserves have dropped to just 16 days’ worth, raising serious concerns about fuel security. This is happening at a time when the Middle East is caus...

item.title: Investigation exposes alleged links between Timor-Leste resort investors and Prince Group network accused of global scams
item.description: An investigation by The Guardian and the Organised Crime and Corruption Reporting Project (OCCRP) has uncovered alleged links between developers of a proposed cryptocurrency resort in Timor-Leste and ...

item.title: Trump threatens further strikes on Iran over Strait of Hormuz blockade
item.description: In a fiery social media outburst, United States President Donald Trump has warned Iran to lift its blockade of the Strait of Hormuz or face a fresh round of military attacks. The post, laden with coar...

item.title: ANC Secretary-General Acknowledges Decline in Public Trust Amid Socioeconomic Challenges
item.description: ANC secretary-general Fikile Mbalula has openly acknowledged that public trust in the party has declined, citing ongoing socioeconomic challenges as key factors. Speaking at the Solomon Mahlangu lectu...

item.title: How AI is reshaping modern propaganda in global conflicts
item.description: The article explores how the conflict involving the United States, Israel, and Iran in the Strait of Hormuz is extending into the digital sphere through the use of artificial intelligence–generated pr...

item.title: Community outraged after Glenhaven Secondary used for Easter gathering involving animal slaughter and property damage
item.description: Residents of Brindhaven in KwaZulu-Natal are furious after Glenhaven Secondary School was reportedly rented out for an Easter religious gathering that left the premises in deplorable condition. Around...

item.title: Alex Eala Prepares for Linz Clay Court Tournament
item.description: Filipino tennis sensation Alex Eala is gearing up for the upcoming clay court tournament in Linz, Austria. The ABS-CBN News report highlights Eala’s preparation and anticipation for this particular st...

item.title: Study Reveals How Recluse Spider Venom Damages Human Cells
item.description: Researchers have uncovered the cellular mechanism behind the dangerous effects of recluse spider venom, which can destroy human tissue. A team led by Alexandra Sundman, in collaboration with Greta Bin...

item.title: Punjab Assembly proposes reforms to abolish outdated poverty-related law
item.description: LAHORE: The Punjab Assembly’s high-level Law Reforms and Delegated Legislation Committee, chaired by Speaker Malik Muhammad Ahmad Khan, has recommended abolishing a colonial-era law that effectively c...

item.title: The History of Conspiracy Theories Surrounding the Founding Fathers and Freemasonry
item.description: This article delves into the historical context surrounding Thomas Jefferson and the Freemasons, revealing how conspiracy theories plagued his reputation and political career. Beginning in the late 18...

item.title: Comelec Reports 3.7 Million Registered Voters for Upcoming Barangay and SK Elections
item.description: As of March 31, 2026, the Commission on Elections (Comelec) has received 3,697,800 applications for voter registration ahead of the May 12 barangay and Sangguniang Kabataan (SK) elections. Calabarzon ...

item.title: Infrastructure as Key to Strengthening Nigeria’s Security and National Cohesion
item.description: This article dey highlight say security for Nigeria no be only matter of police or military force. Dr. Lazarus Angbazo talk say infrastructure fit actually reduce insecurity if e properly deployed. E ...

item.title: Young Consultant Purchases First Condo in Vancouver Independently at Age 24
item.description: At just 24, Stephanie achieved what many consider nearly impossible: buying her first home in Vancouver, one of Canada’s most expensive cities, without financial assistance from her family. Now 25, sh...

item.title: Audit Reveals Poor Conditions in Kenya’s Correctional Facilities Despite Reforms
item.description: An audit by Auditor-General Nancy Gathungu has revealed serious issues within Kenya’s correctional facilities, highlighting the failure of the government to implement effective reforms in the penal sy...

item.title: US Officials Engage Pakistan Intermediaries as Trump Sets Deadline for Iran Deal
item.description: US President Donald Trump, on Monday, issued stern warnings to Iran, stating that the country could be neutralised in one night if it fails to reach an agreement with the United States by Tuesday nigh...

item.title: Megyn Kelly discusses the benefits of hormone replacement therapy for menopausal health
item.description: Megyn Kelly, aged 55, has openly shared her positive experience with hormone replacement therapy (HRT) during menopause, emphasising that there is no shame in ageing. Speaking on her SiriusXM show wit...

item.title: Former 'PBB Collab 2.0' housemates open up about fame and keeping life grounded
item.description: The article tackles the down-to-earth personalities of the former 'Pinoy Big Brother Collab 2.0' housemates, who proved that fame has not changed their humility and approach to life. Written by Liezel...

item.title: Lincoln City promoted to the Championship after 65 years
item.description: Lincoln City have achieved promotion to the Championship, marking their first return to the English second tier in 65 years. The decisive moment came with a thrilling 2-1 victory over Reading, secured...

item.title: Kerala Revises Its Position in Supreme Court Review of Sabarimala Entry Case
item.description: The Kerala government has shifted its stance in the long-standing controversy surrounding the Sabarimala temple entry issue. In its new submission to the Supreme Court during the review hearing, the s...
